content:
- Completed Task 04 execution: src/logging/mod.rs now routes typed LogRecord emissions through a tracing-backed adapter while preserving the existing Result-returning emission contract and explicit fanout failure semantics.
- Added tracing/tracing-subscriber deps, kept the existing JSONL stderr/file writers as the deterministic formatting layer underneath the adapter, updated logging tests, and updated operator docs/task text to state that OTEL is explicitly deferred for now.
- All required gates passed: make check, make test (428 passed, 9 skipped by profile filter), make test-long (9/9 passed), and make lint.
- I ran .ralph/task_switch.sh already. Next I only need to git add everything, commit with the required task finished prefix including the gate evidence and implementation summary, push, and then stop immediately.

- Read .ralph/current_tasks.md and .ralph/current_tasks_done.md plus the active task files themselves.
- Confirmed there are no active failing bug tasks; all bugs in current_tasks_done are <passes>true</passes>.
- Checked active candidates for <blocked_by>; none of the active task files contain blocker tags, so the current failing non-bug tasks are all unblocked.
- Compared priorities: only active explicit priority is low for the magic-number cleanup, while the meta-task is only eligible when all failing tasks are gone.
- Chose the next task from the docs usefulness story: task 01 is the best foundation because task 04 explicitly says it belongs at the end of that story, task 03 is a structural cleanup that is safer after the content direction is rewritten, and task 01 improves the operator-facing docs before deeper/contributor/readme follow-ons.
- Next action is to write .ralph/tasks/story-docs-useful-guides/01-task-rewrite-operator-docs-as-useful-user-guides.md into .ralph/current_task.txt and quit immediately.

- Read previous progress and current task pointer.
- Opened task 01-task-rewrite-operator-docs-as-useful-user-guides.md and confirmed it is still at initial state with no TO BE VERIFIED/NOW EXECUTE marker.
- Next I need to inspect the current docs structure and enough implementation context to write a detailed execution plan into the task file, then append TO BE VERIFIED and stop immediately.

- Explored the docs tree, summary, operator pages, quick-start pages, interfaces pages, and the runtime/config/API/CLI sources needed to ground a plan.
- Found concrete mismatches to drive the eventual rewrite: verification pages still live in SUMMARY, operator pages still use mechanical template sections, CLI docs show the wrong switchover command, and the docs hide a CLI default base URL mismatch against the runtime API default.
- Wrote a detailed execution plan into task 01-task-rewrite-operator-docs-as-useful-user-guides.md, ended it with TO BE VERIFIED, and must stop now so the next turn can perform the required skeptical review step.

- Read the existing task plan and confirmed the file is in TO BE VERIFIED state, so this turn is the skeptical review step rather than initial planning.
- Found at least two concrete problems with the old plan: it referenced the wrong summary file path (`docs/src/SUMMARY.md`, not `docs/SUMMARY.md`), and it did not explicitly account for contributor pages that currently link into the soon-to-be-removed verification section.
- Next I need to revise the task plan in the task file with those corrections, make at least one substantive plan change, replace TO BE VERIFIED with NOW EXECUTE, and then stop so the next turn can execute without re-exploring.

- Revised the task plan during the skeptical review and replaced TO BE VERIFIED with NOW EXECUTE.
- The concrete plan changes were: add contributor cross-link cleanup after deleting the verification section, and expand the rewrite scope to the lifecycle pages that remain in the operator reading path and still use the bad template structure.
- Stopping now exactly at the handoff point the workflow requires; the next turn should execute the written plan directly without re-exploring.

- Read the Ralph progress log and current task pointer at startup.
- The progress log says the prior turn already completed skeptical review and replaced TO BE VERIFIED with NOW EXECUTE for the operator docs rewrite task.
- Next I need to confirm the task file contents, then execute the written plan directly without re-exploring.

- Confirmed the task file is in NOW EXECUTE state and started execution.
- Read the quick-start/operator/lifecycle docs plus CLI/config/API/TLS code to ground the rewrite in real defaults and commands.
- Found the key factual constraints to carry into the edits: runtime API default listen address is 127.0.0.1:8080, CLI default base URL is still http://127.0.0.1:8008, and switchover goes through `ha switchover request --requested-by`.

- Working slice 3 for the operator docs rewrite execution.
- Confirmed the real API routes and the CLI mismatch: docs showed the wrong switchover command, and the CLI default base URL still points at 127.0.0.1:8008 while the runtime default API listen address is 127.0.0.1:8080.
- Next I will patch the CLI default to 8080, rewrite docs/src/interfaces/cli.md and docs/src/interfaces/node-api.md around the real commands/routes/security model, and then report the changed files back to the parent agent.

- Confirmed the task file is already in NOW EXECUTE state, so this turn is the execution pass.
- Spawned three worker agents on disjoint doc slices: navigation/removal, quick-start+operator+lifecycle rewrites, and interfaces/code-doc reconciliation.
- Locally verified the key implementation facts the docs must match: runtime API default listen address is 127.0.0.1:8080, CLI default base_url is still http://127.0.0.1:8008, switchover is `ha switchover request --requested-by`, and API security/auth schema requires explicit v2 blocks.
- Next I need to merge the workers' edits, decide whether to fix the CLI default in code or document an explicit override, then run the full required gates and do Ralph closeout.

- Patched src/cli/args.rs so the CLI default base URL now matches the runtime default API listen address: http://127.0.0.1:8080.
- Rewrote docs/src/interfaces/cli.md and docs/src/interfaces/node-api.md around the real command tree, actual routes, role-token auth, TLS expectations, and the accepted switchover request payload.
- I hit a malformed cargo test invocation and corrected it immediately; next I just need the targeted parser tests to finish and then I can hand the changed-file summary back to the parent agent.

- Finished the slice-3 edits and the targeted CLI parser tests all passed.
- I am doing one last repo-wide grep for the old CLI default URL and obsolete switchover syntax so the integration pass knows whether any stale references remain outside my owned files.
- If that grep comes back clean, I can report this slice as complete with no blockers.

- Re-checked the CLI source and found the base URL default is already changed locally to http://127.0.0.1:8080, with the matching unit test updated too.
- That is the cleaner reconciliation for the docs task because it removes the misleading default instead of teaching operators to override it.
- Waiting on worker outputs now, then I need to review and integrate their concrete file changes before running all gates.

- The interfaces/code reconciliation slice completed and its edits are already present in the main workspace: src/cli/args.rs plus rewritten docs/src/interfaces/{cli,node-api}.md.
- Remaining worker slices for navigation/removal and quick-start/operator/lifecycle rewrites are still running; they own the majority of the book rewrite.
- If they do not finish soon, I should complete those remaining doc files locally rather than stall, then run the full gates.

- Executed the docs rewrite plan: removed the verification appendix from SUMMARY and deleted the stale verification report pages.
- Rewrote introduction, docs map, quick-start, operator, and lifecycle pages into direct operator prose, and aligned the CLI default base URL with the runtime API default at 127.0.0.1:8080.
- Next I need to finish the coherence pass, run mdBook plus the required make targets, then update the task file checkboxes and Ralph closeout fields only if every gate passes.

- Rewrote the start-here, quick-start, interfaces, contributor verification, operator, and lifecycle docs as full-file replacements to avoid patch drift.
- Corrected the CLI/API alignment around the default API port and rewrote switchover docs so they describe the actual untargeted `requested_by` contract instead of a nonexistent target-member CLI.
- Next I need to remove the stale verification pages from disk, do a coherence sweep for leftover filler or broken cross-links, and then run the full required gates.

- Finished the main docs rewrite pass locally: secure-by-default configuration guide, operator/deployment/observability/troubleshooting, lifecycle pages, and remaining non-contributor pages that still used the mechanical template headings.
- Confirmed with rg that stale `switchover --to` / `switchover cancel` syntax and the forced operator-facing template headings are gone; only contributor-facing tradeoff sections remain.
- Next I am running the full required gates in order: make check, make test, make test-long, make lint.

- All required gates passed after one docs-lint fix: make check, make test, make test-long, and make lint.
- Updated the task file to mark the acceptance criteria complete and set passes=true.
- Next I need to inspect the remaining modified files, run .ralph/task_switch.sh, then commit and push every tracked change required by the Ralph workflow.
